title: Japanese-Style Pilaf Pastry Triangles
description: A fusion of Ukrainian and Japanese flavors, these flaky pastry triangles are filled with a savory pilaf mixture.

ingredients.name: Ukrainian Pilaf
ingredients.notes: or Japanese-style pilaf with added Japanese seasonings

instructions: 2. Roll out the golden pastry to a thickness of about 1/8 inch (3 mm).
3. In a small bowl, mix together the Ukrainian pilaf, soy sauce, rice vinegar, scallions, sesame seeds, salt, and black pepper.
4. Place the pilaf mixture onto one half of the pastry, leaving a 1/2 inch (1 cm) border around the edges.
5. Brush the edges with a little water, then fold the other half of the pastry over the filling to form a triangle.
6. Press the edges to seal, then use a fork to crimp them.
7. Brush the tops of the triangles with a little water, then sprinkle with sesame seeds if desired.
8. Bake for 20-25 minutes, or until the pastry is golden brown.
9. Serve warm or at room temperature.
